charity.s: $5M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.11% of revenue) via Arby's Foundation.
charity.focus: Youth empowerment, hunger relief, career development

labor.s: 1 OSHA severe-injury reports in past 2y (0 amputations); 5 since 2015.

animals.s: NRDC Chain Reaction: F — no public antibiotic-stewardship policy. [NRDC Chain Reaction F]

wiki.extract: Arby's Restaurant Group, Inc. doing business as Arby's is an American fast food sandwich restaurant chain with more than 3,300 restaurants. The flagship property of Inspire Brands, it ranked third in systemwide sales in the United States in the quick-service and fast-casual restaurant industries in 2012, behind Subway and Panera Bread. In October 2017, Food & Wine called Arby's "America's second largest sandwich chain ".

products: Arby's Adventure Meals Musical Notes toy saxophones

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was entering the facility when they slipped and fell on the floor. The employee sustained a broken left arm requiring surgery.
One employee shot another after an altercation. The employee who was shot suffered a broken left arm and artery damage and was hospitalized.

An employee was training another employee on how to clean the shake machine when the shake machine expelled excess cleaning solution onto an electrical outlet. The employee unplugged the shake machine's cord from the outlet and was shocked.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Back, including spine, spinal cord, unspecified
sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 16 to 20 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was changing letters on a reader board while standing on an 18-foot extension ladder when he fell to the grassy ground below and landed on his back. He bruised his spinal cord and required hospitalization.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping on uneven surface
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped over the drainage system and fell breaking the left leg.
